title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
summary: Cole Medin breaks down Google's whitepaper on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model is only 10% of success while the surrounding harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) comprises the critical 90%. The video frames AI coding as a spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering and emphasizes skills in context engineering, harness development, and the shift from conductor to orchestrator roles.
writeup: Cole Medin walks through Google's whitepaper on agentic engineering, centering on the claim that the AI model accounts for roughly 10% of success while the surrounding harness (context, tools, guardrails, and verification) accounts for the critical 90%. The video positions AI coding as a spectrum running from vibe coding to agentic engineering, and stresses skills in context engineering and harness development. It also describes a shift in the developer's role from conductor to orchestrator.
